families.proof: Rust post-opt56 authority median qps is materially above refreshed native BF16 qps at near-equal recall on the same remote x86 benchmark surface.

families.blocker: IVF-PQ is already archived as no-go, so it cannot satisfy the final performance-leadership criterion.
DiskANN remains constrained and its benchmark lane is not a trusted native-comparable leadership surface.

summary: The final performance leadership criterion is met on the current remote x86 authority surface because HNSW now has a credible leadership win over native at near-equal recall on the same benchmark surface.
action: Archive the performance-leadership proof as met and keep future reopen work evidence-based: any downgrade or replacement claim must come from new authority artifacts.
